The '''Golan III Space Defense NovaGun''', also known simply as the '''Golan III''', was the third [[Space station/Legends|space station]] in [[Golan Arms/Legends|Golan Arms]]' series of [[Golan space defense platform|space defense platforms]].

==Description==
Built as a more powerful successor to the dangerous [[Golan II Space Defense SpaceGun|Golan II]], the Golan III was larger and arguably better armed than an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]]. Just as the Golan II had stronger [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] and a sturdier hull than the [[Golan I Space Defense Platform/Legends|Golan I]], the Golan III had shields at least 25% stronger than the Golan II and a similar increase in hull durability.

They were armed with 50 [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, 24 [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] launchers, and 15 [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors, and had a hangar capable of holding at least 12 [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]].

==History==
Golan III platforms were assigned to the most valuable locations in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. One notable use of Golan III platforms was their deployment as [[Gauntlet Scanner Station|scanner stations]] in the [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] Security Zone, working in concert with [[Allegiance-class battlecruiser|''Allegiance''-class battlecruisers]].

Multiple Golan IIIs formed part of the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant's]] [[Coruscant Defense Fleet/Legends|Defense]] [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]] during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]]. Many of them would be destroyed when the New Republic [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|took]] Coruscant from [[Director of Imperial Intelligence|Director]] [[Ysanne Isard]].

They were also stationed above many other valuable worlds, such as [[Zhar system]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]], remaining in use through the end of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] and beyond.
